Package microui.core.style

Class AbstractLerpedColor

java.lang.Object
microui.core.style.AbstractColor
microui.core.style.AbstractLerpedColor
Direct Known Subclasses:
LerpedColor, LerpedLoopColor
public abstract class AbstractLerpedColor extends AbstractColor
Abstract class for all classes which releases interpolation of colors

  • Field Summary

    Fields inherited from class microui.core.style.AbstractColor

    MAX_VALUE, MIN_VALUE

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    AbstractLerpedColor(Supplier<AbstractColor> start, Supplier<AbstractColor> end)
    Constructs a lerped color with dynamic start and end colors supplied by the given suppliers.
    AbstractLerpedColor(AbstractColor start, AbstractColor end)
    Constructs a lerped color with fixed start and end colors.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    int
    getAlpha()
    Returns the alpha component of the interpolated color.

    Returns the blue component of the interpolated color.

    getGreen()
    Returns the green component of the interpolated color.

    getRed()
    Returns the red component of the interpolated color.
    final float
    getSpeed()
    Returns the current animation speed.

    Methods inherited from class microui.core.style.AbstractColor

    apply, apply, applyBackground, applyBackground, applyStroke, applyStroke, applyTint, applyTint

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractLerpedColor

      public AbstractLerpedColor(AbstractColor start, AbstractColor end)
      Constructs a lerped color with fixed start and end colors.
      Parameters:
      start - the start color (must not be null)
      end - the end color (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if either argument is null

    • AbstractLerpedColor

      public AbstractLerpedColor(Supplier<AbstractColor> start, Supplier<AbstractColor> end)
      Constructs a lerped color with dynamic start and end colors supplied by the given suppliers. The suppliers are called each time the color components are needed, allowing the start/end colors to change over time.
      Parameters:
      start - supplier for the start color (must not be null)
      end - supplier for the end color (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if either supplier is null

  • Method Details

    • getRed

      public int getRed()
      Returns the red component of the interpolated color. The value is calculated based on the current animation progress.
      Specified by:
      getRed in class AbstractColor
      Returns:
      the interpolated red component value (0-255)

    • getGreen

      public int getGreen()
      Returns the green component of the interpolated color. The value is calculated based on the current animation progress.
      Specified by:
      getGreen in class AbstractColor
      Returns:
      the interpolated green component value (0-255)

    • getBlue

      public int getBlue()
      Returns the blue component of the interpolated color. The value is calculated based on the current animation progress.
      Specified by:
      getBlue in class AbstractColor
      Returns:
      the interpolated blue component value (0-255)

    • getAlpha

      public int getAlpha()
      Returns the alpha component of the interpolated color. The value is calculated based on the current animation progress.
      Specified by:
      getAlpha in class AbstractColor
      Returns:
      the interpolated alpha component value (0-255)

    • getSpeed

      public final float getSpeed()
      Returns the current animation speed. The speed determines how quickly the interpolation progresses between the start and end colors during each update.
      Returns:
      the current animation speed, a value between 0.0 and 1.0

    • setSpeed

      public final AbstractLerpedColor setSpeed(float speed)
      Sets the animation speed and returns this instance for method chaining. The speed determines how quickly the interpolation progresses between the start and end colors during each update.
      Parameters:
      speed - the new animation speed, must be between 0.0 and 1.0
      Returns:
      this instance for method chaining
